title: 10 best herbal creams for psoriasis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The aloe vera gel in these creams has been shown to calm irritated skin and promote healing, helping to alleviate symptoms such as itching and burning. Additionally, aloe's natural moisturizing effects can help hydrate dry, flaky skin, improving its overall texture and appearance. This multi-faceted approach makes aloe creams an effective treatment option for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis by reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's natural moisturizing properties can help soothe dry, scaly skin associated with psoriasis by locking in moisture and creating a protective barrier that prevents further irritation and dryness.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe creams may also contain antioxidant compounds that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, both of which are involved in the development of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Leaves: Aloe leaves are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that soothe and calm the skin, making them an ideal choice for psoriasis cream formulations.
remedy_recipe: Gather aloe vera gel and add it to a bowl with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Combine the mixture with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and heat it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool before adding 1 teaspoon of chamomile essential oil.
Mix well and then whip the cream using an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes until stiff peaks form.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with psoriasis because they contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriatic lesions, alleviating discomfort and itchiness. The cream's natural antioxidants also promote skin regeneration and collagen synthesis, fostering a more even tone and texture. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects help to calm irritated skin, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream helps reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis, which can lead to redness, swelling, and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to psoriasis symptoms such as scaling and flaking.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Pot marigold's wound-healing properties can aid in the repair of damaged skin tissue, while its soothing properties provide relief from itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritation associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and essential amino acids, they help to reduce inflammation, promote skin elasticity, and improve skin barrier function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers using low heat and air circulation to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine one part dried calendula flowers with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over medium heat for 20-30 minutes or until the calendula infuses into the oil, then strain through cheesecloth.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and emulsifiers to create a creamy consistency, allowing the mixture to cool and thicken before pouring into containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory, soothing, and calming properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis lesions, providing instant relief to itchy, inflamed skin. Additionally, chamomile's antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote healthy healing, allowing the skin to regenerate and recover from the condition. As a result, chamomile creams can effectively alleviate psoriasis symptoms, promoting a smoother, more comfortable skin texture.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage and inflammation in psoriasis, promoting overall skin health and reducing symptoms.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's apigenin can bind to GABA receptors in the skin, producing a calming effect that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for psoriasis flares.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritation associated with psoriasis.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and other flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, relieve itching, and improve skin hydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality chamomilla recutita flowers of optimal potency in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried chamomilla recutita flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 20 grams of powdered chamomilla recutita flowers in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and infuse for one hour before whipping it into a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with psoriasis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of this chronic skin condition. Curcumin reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis. Additionally, turmeric's natural antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections often linked to psoriasis flare-ups, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of plaques and scales.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant effects: The antioxidant properties of turmeric cream help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of psoriasis.

Immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric's immunomodulatory properties help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis triggers, reducing inflammation and slowing down the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the turmeric plant are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality curcuma longa roots and dry them thoroughly for future use.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of curcuma longa powder with 20 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until a smooth paste forms.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with psoriasis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The creams also have antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, ginger's warming properties increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected areas, allowing for faster healing and reduced itching and scaling. This holistic approach to skincare can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms without harsh chemicals or steroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of psoriasis like redness, swelling, and itching.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to psoriasis development.

Antipruritic properties: Ginger's ability to reduce inflammation and itching can provide relief from the intense pruritus (itching) associated with psoriasis, making it a valuable natural remedy for soothing skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of oleoresin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ginger root and clean it thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned ginger root in a warm place for 7-10 days or until it becomes brittle.
Grind 20g of dried ginger powder into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 100ml of coconut oil with 50g of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the grated ginger paste with the melted oil and wax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, are absorbed into the skin, where they target immune cells that contribute to psoriasis inflammation. This results in a soothing and calming effect on the affected areas, leading to improved symptoms and reduced appearance of plaques and scales.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing in psoriasis patients.

Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that can help reduce moisture loss from the skin, which is often compromised in psoriasis patients, and also provide antioxidant benefits to protect against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and preventing skin cell growth.
Fruits: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and calm psoriatic skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500mg of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a reputable source.
Combine the dried herb with 10g of petroleum jelly in a mortar and pestle and grind into a fine powder for 30 seconds.
Add 5ml of sweet almond oil to the powdered mixture and mix until it forms a smooth paste for 1 minute.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65°c for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter and transfer the infused oil to sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with psoriasis because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The mucilage present in marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and itching, providing relief from discomfort and pain. This natural remedy can be used topically to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, promoting healthier and clearer skin.
remedy_properties: Emollient and Humectant Properties: Herbal marshmallow creams can help soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis. This is because they form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and shielding it from environmental irritants.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: The mucilages present in herbal marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis. This is because they inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage.

Soothing and Calming Properties: Herbal marshmallow creams contain soothing compounds that can calm irritated skin and reduce the risk of infection in psoriasis lesions. This is because they have anti-irritant properties, which help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and calm inflamed skin, making them effective in reducing symptoms of psoriasis.
Rhizomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis for the infusion process.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature environment for 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried marshmallow root with 1000 milliliters of boiling water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids.
Mix the strained infusion with 200 grams of unscented coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax, heating until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, penetrate deep into the skin to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis lesions. Additionally, comfrey's mucilages provide a protective barrier, shielding the skin from environmental stressors and promoting wound healing. As a result, comfrey creams can help calm and calm down inflamed areas, reducing the appearance of psoriatic plaques and improving overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Helps to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and accelerate tissue regeneration by stimulating collagen synthesis, which can aid in reducing psoriasis symptoms such as redness, swelling, and skin lesions.
Alkaloids: Have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with psoriasis, as well as alleviate itching and scaling.

Polysaccharides: Possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, which can help to reduce oxidative stress, modulate the immune response, and prevent tissue damage, ultimately contributing to improved skin health in individuals with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Buds: Contains rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried symphytum officinale root, carefully cleaning and cutting it into small pieces.
Mix the symphytum officinale root with 40 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating for 2 hours.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax pellets with the cooled herb-coconut oil mixture, stirring until fully incorporated.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the mixture, blending thoroughly.
Pour the finished cream into sterilized containers, allowing it to cool and harden for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with psoriasis because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with the condition. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to effectively calm red, itchy skin and promote a sense of relaxation. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream can help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin, providing relief from psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to skin damage and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce itchiness, redness, and swelling associated with psoriasis, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antifungal and antibacterial properties may aid in wound healing by reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin regeneration, which is beneficial for managing psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm psoriatic skin.
Fruits: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration, making them beneficial for treating psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in bulk and measure out 20 grams of the herb.
Grind the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and strain it through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 1 hour.
Whip the liquid mixture with an electric whisk for 10 minutes until it thickens to a creamy consistency, then pour into jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with psoriasis because of its unique combination of antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The tea tree oil present in these creams has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin, while also preventing bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate psoriatic symptoms. Additionally, the antioxidants in tea tree cream help to combat oxidative stress, which is often associated with chronic conditions like psoriasis. Overall, tea tree creams offer a natural and effective way to manage psoriasis symptoms and promote healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Tee Tree Oil (Melaleuca Alternifolia): Has antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in psoriatic lesions, promoting faster healing.
Aloe Vera: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.

Lavender Oil (Lavandula Angustifolia): Has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce itching, scaling, and inflammation, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-related triggers of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds such as terpenoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.

Leaves: Tea tree leaves are rich in essential oils like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and alpha-pinene, which possess antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100 grams of distilled water and add 10 grams of grated beeswax.
Combine the mixture in a heat-resistant glass bowl and place it in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Remove from heat and add 20 drops of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and let cool for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with psoriasis because of its unique ability to soothe and calm inflamed skin. The natural anti-inflammatory properties in plantain work to reduce redness and swelling, allowing for a smoother, more even tone. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of plantain cream help to lock in hydration and protect the skin from further irritation. This gentle and non-irritating approach makes it an excellent alternative to harsh topical treatments, providing long-lasting relief for psoriasis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis, providing relief to affected skin.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in plantain cream neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to psoriasis flares, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing.

Softenening and moisturizing effects: Plantain cream's moisturizing properties help soothe dry, scaly skin associated with psoriasis, while its softening effects reduce irritation and discomfort caused by rough patches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm psoriatic skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend 100g of fresh leaves with 200ml of distilled water in a blender until smooth and pulpy.
Steep the plant mixture in 400ml of hot water for 30 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 80g of coconut oil and 10ml of the plantago major infusion in a double boiler until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's beta-carotene content is converted to vitamin A in the skin, which reduces inflammation and promotes cell turnover, helping to clear up scales and plaque buildup associated with psoriasis. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant activity neutralizes free radicals that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. By soothing and calming the skin, dandelion creams provide relief from itching, redness, and irritation, offering a natural solution for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with psoriasis, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healthier skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants in dandelion cream neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to cell damage and inflammation that exacerbates psoriasis symptoms, protecting the skin from further harm.
Immunomodulatory: Dandelion's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune response, reducing overactive T-cell activity that can lead to psoriasis flares and promoting a balanced immune system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritation associated with psoriasis.

Fruits: Dandelion fruits contain a variety of bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to calm down the immune system's overactive response in psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from your garden or local park, collecting approximately two cups of each.
Combine the dried dandelion flowers and leaves in a heat-proof bowl, adding one cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and let it steep for six hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible, then discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of beeswax and half teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the herbal infusion, heating the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts and emulsifies.
Remove from heat, let it cool slightly before pouring into small glass containers and refrigerating for at least two weeks or up to six months for the cream to mature and thicken fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with psoriasis because they contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin. The creams also contain saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the buildup of excess skin cells, a common symptom of psoriasis. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties may help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote overall skin health, leading to reduced symptoms of psoriasis and improved skin clarity.
remedy_properties: Isolation of Bioactive Compounds: Red clover creams contain bioactive compounds such as isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.

Anti-Inflammatory Activity: The polyphenols present in red clover creams exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing the severity of psoriatic lesions and alleviating discomfort associated with itching, burning, and scaling.

Immunomodulatory Effects: Red clover creams have been shown to modulate immune responses, suppressing T-cell activation and cytokine production, which helps to reduce inflammation and prevent further tissue damage in psoriatic skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe inflammation and reduce skin irritation associated with psoriasis.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and isoflavones, which promote skin health and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense leaves and dry them for at least two hours in the shade.
Blend one tablespoon of dried trifolium pratense leaves with two tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan over low heat for 20 minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Mix one teaspoon of beeswax with three teaspoons of coconut cream and heat the mixture until melted.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined before pouring into containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with psoriasis because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called valerenic acid, which soothes and calms the skin. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to lock in hydration, reducing dryness and itchiness associated with psoriasis. Additionally, valerian's antimicrobial properties may help to reduce bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate psoriatic lesions. By targeting multiple aspects of psoriasis symptoms, valerian creams can provide effective relief and support for individuals suffering from this chronic skin condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian cream helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to psoriasis development and progression.
Immunomodulatory properties: Valerian cream can modulate the immune system's response, which helps reduce excessive immune activity that can lead to psoriasis flares.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with psoriasis.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a unique blend of flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for psoriasis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks.
Grind dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 200 grams of the ground root powder with 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and let it cool before transferring it to an airtight container.
Allow the cream to mature at room temperature for four weeks before using it, shaking the container daily to ensure even infusion of the herbal ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The essential oils present in sage, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin, common symptoms associated with psoriasis. Additionally, sage's antioxidants help to combat oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. By reducing redness, itching, and inflammation, sage creams provide relief from the discomfort and appearance of psoriasis patches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Sage cream's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key contributors to psoriasis symptoms.

Antimicrobial and antifungal properties: Sage cream's antimicrobial and antifungal properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate psoriasis, reducing the risk of secondary infections and promoting wound healing.

Wound-healing and tissue repair properties: Sage cream's rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids can stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and accelerate tissue repair, helping to reduce scarring and promote healthy skin regeneration in psoriasis lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain salves that can help to reduce redness and swelling, while also providing antimicrobial protection against infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried salvia officinalis leaves and mix them with 20 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Combine the mixture with 30 milliliters of coconut oil and stir until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before adding 5 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Whisk the mixture thoroughly for 10 minutes to ensure all ingredients are well combined and emulsified.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a refrigerator at 5 degrees celsius for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy skin, providing relief from the discomfort associated with psoriasis. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. By applying a thyme cream topically, individuals can experience improved skin hydration, reduced redness, and a noticeable decrease in psoriasis lesions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Thyme contains anti-inflammatory compounds like thymol, carvacrol, and geraniol, which can help reduce inflammation in the skin, a key symptom of psoriasis.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of thyme cream can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to or worsen psoriasis, promoting a healthier skin environment.

Wound-healing activity: Thyme has been shown to stimulate wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and enhancing the proliferation of fibroblasts, which can help reduce scarring and improve the appearance of skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Thyme barks have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including psoriasis, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from your garden or store-dried thyme in airtight containers.
Combine 200g of dried thyme with 500ml of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours allowing the thyme to infuse its properties into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and reserve liquid.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax and shea butter in a 1:2 ratio at a temperature of around 60°c for about an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with psoriasis because they contain rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that soothes and calms inflamed skin. This herb has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and itching associated with psoriasis, promoting a sense of relief for affected individuals. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial effects help to combat bacterial and fungal infections often found in psoriatic lesions, further aiding in the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm cream reduces inflammation, a primary contributor to psoriasis symptoms like redness, swelling, and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis.
Antimicrobial: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to skin infections and inflammation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with psoriasis.

Leaves: With their high content of flavonoids and rosmarinic acid, lemon balm leaves possess potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce redness and itching caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality melissa officinalis leaves for maximum potency and freshness.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 6 ounces of cold-pressed coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Remove from heat and allow the cream to cool and solidify before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed creams helps with psoriasis because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The natural oils in chickweed, such as triterpenoids, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, chickweed's anti-itch properties help alleviate the uncomfortable sensations often experienced by those with psoriasis, providing relief from constant itching and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, chickweed creams can help to improve symptoms of psoriasis and promote healthier, more balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chickweed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and triterpenes, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of chickweed help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and inflammation, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing the appearance of psoriatic lesions.

Soothing and moisturizing agents: Chickweed creams often contain soothing and moisturizing agents like glycerin, aloe vera, and chamomile, which can help calm irritated skin, lock in moisture, and reduce flakiness associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chickweed roots contain high amounts of mucilage, which provides soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for relieving redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.

Buds: The buds of the chickweed plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin health, making them a valuable addition to creams for psoriasis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stellaria media flowers and leaves then dry them completely in the shade for two weeks.
Combine dried stellaria media flowers and leaves with coconut oil in a 2:1 ratio then soak mixture overnight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth then discard solids and transfer infused oil to a double boiler.
Add beeswax and shea butter to the infused oil then melt mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat let cool and thicken then store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with psoriasis because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work together to soothe itchy skin, reduce redness and swelling, and prevent the formation of scaly plaques. Additionally, witch hazel's astringent and antibacterial properties help to unclog pores and reduce inflammation, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and reducing psoriasis symptoms such as flaking and cracking.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis by constricting blood vessels and reducing histamine release, which in turn reduces redness and swelling.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in witch hazel have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of psoriatic lesions and preventing infection.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid has potent antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and tissue repair in psoriasis-affected areas.
remedy_parts: Bark: Rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm psoriatic skin.
Roots: Contain gallic acid, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and stems from the hamamelis virginiana plant weighing approximately 200 grams.
Dry the witch hazel plant material in a warm, dark place for seven to ten days or until it reaches a moisture level of 10%.
Crush the dried witch hazel plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle taking approximately one hour.

Combine the witch hazel powder with 400 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler stirring until the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c after approximately two hours.
Strain the cooled herbal cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass jars using gravity and let it mature for at least three weeks before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for psoriasis are topical treatments that combine natural ingredients, such as herbs and botanicals, to provide relief from the symptoms of psoriasis. These creams help to soothe dry skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing by targeting the root causes of the condition. Examples of herbal creams that show promise in managing psoriasis include creams containing aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which can help alleviate itching, scaling, and redness. By using these natural remedies, individuals with psoriasis can experience improved quality of life, reduced discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal creams is by using natural ingredients that soothe and calm the skin. Look for creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Apply the cream topically to affected areas twice daily, gently massaging it into the skin until fully absorbed. Regular use can help reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, dermatitis, and atopic dermatitis. These conditions cause inflammation, itching, and skin lesions, which herbal creams can help soothe and calm. Creams containing herbs like aloe vera, calendula, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, providing relief to affected individuals.
